title: NOC 51122 – Musicians and Singers | GTR Immigration
description: Musicians and Singers under NOC 51122 are professional performing artists responsible for delivering live and recorded musical performances across Canada’s arts and entertainment industries. Classified under TEER 1, this occupation usually requires formal musical training and demonstrated performance ability. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group51 – Professional occupations in art and cultureSub-Major Group511 – Professional occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5112 – Creative and performing artistsNOC Code51122

duties: Perform as soloists or members of musical groups
Play one or more instruments in live or recorded settings
Rehearse and collaborate with other performers
Perform musical arrangements as soloists or in vocal groups
Participate in rehearsals and recording sessions
Provide instruction in musical technique and interpretation
Teach music theory and performance skills
employment_requirements: Musical training through a university, college, conservatory, or private instruction is usually required
Professional orchestral musicians and classical singers typically require a university degree in music
Audition performance is a key hiring criterion
Membership in a guild or union may be required depending on performance type
salary_prospects: $27,500 – $88,100 per year,depending on performance scale, specialization, and employment type.
With 44,500 projected job openings compared to 37,100 job seekers, NOC 51122 reflects strong demand across Canada’s entertainment industry.
Growth of live music events and festivals
Opportunities are strongest in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Quebec, and Alberta.

labour_market_demand: Growth of Canada’s live entertainment industry
Expansion of streaming and digital music platforms
Replacement demand due to workforce mobility
With significantly more job openings than job seekers projected, employment prospects are considered strong.

Musicians and Singers play a central role in Canada’s creative and cultural economy.
